1001: // HasSubCommands determines if the command has children commands.
1002: func (c *Command) HasSubCommands() bool {
1003: 	return len(c.commands) > 0
1004: }
1005: 
1006: // IsAvailableCommand determines if a command is available as a non-help command
1007: // (this includes all non deprecated/hidden commands).
1008: func (c *Command) IsAvailableCommand() bool {
1009: 	if len(c.Deprecated) != 0 || c.Hidden {
1010: 		return false
1011: 	}
1012: 
1013: 	if c.HasParent() && c.Parent().helpCommand == c {
1014: 		return false
1015: 	}
1016: 
1017: 	if c.Runnable() || c.HasAvailableSubCommands() {
1018: 		return true
1019: 	}
1020: 
1021: 	return false
1022: }
1023: 
1024: // IsHelpCommand determines if a command is a 'help' command; a help command is
1025: // determined by the fact that it is NOT runnable/hidden/deprecated, and has no
1026: // sub commands that are runnable/hidden/deprecated.
1027: func (c *Command) IsHelpCommand() bool {
1028: 
1029: 	// if a command is runnable, deprecated, or hidden it is not a 'help' command
1030: 	if c.Runnable() || len(c.Deprecated) != 0 || c.Hidden {
1031: 		return false
1032: 	}
1033: 
1034: 	// if any non-help sub commands are found, the command is not a 'help' command
1035: 	for _, sub := range c.commands {
1036: 		if !sub.IsHelpCommand() {
1037: 			return false
1038: 		}
1039: 	}
1040: 
1041: 	// the command either has no sub commands, or no non-help sub commands
1042: 	return true
1043: }
1044: 
1045: // HasHelpSubCommands determines if a command has any available 'help' sub commands
1046: // that need to be shown in the usage/help default template under 'additional help
1047: // topics'.
1048: func (c *Command) HasHelpSubCommands() bool {
1049: 
1050: 	// return true on the first found available 'help' sub command
1051: 	for _, sub := range c.commands {
1052: 		if sub.IsHelpCommand() {
1053: 			return true
1054: 		}
1055: 	}
1056: 
1057: 	// the command either has no sub commands, or no available 'help' sub commands
1058: 	return false
1059: }
1060: 
1061: // HasAvailableSubCommands determines if a command has available sub commands that
1062: // need to be shown in the usage/help default template under 'available commands'.
1063: func (c *Command) HasAvailableSubCommands() bool {
1064: 
1065: 	// return true on the first found available (non deprecated/help/hidden)
1066: 	// sub command
1067: 	for _, sub := range c.commands {
1068: 		if sub.IsAvailableCommand() {
1069: 			return true
1070: 		}
1071: 	}
1072: 
1073: 	// the command either has no sub comamnds, or no available (non deprecated/help/hidden)
1074: 	// sub commands
1075: 	return false
1076: }
1077: 
1078: // HasParent determines if the command is a child command.
1079: func (c *Command) HasParent() bool {
1080: 	return c.parent != nil
1081: }
1082: 
1083: // GlobalNormalizationFunc returns the global normalization function or nil if doesn't exists.
1084: func (c *Command) GlobalNormalizationFunc() func(f *flag.FlagSet, name string) flag.NormalizedName {
1085: 	return c.globNormFunc
1086: }
1087: 
1088: // Flags returns the complete FlagSet that applies
1089: // to this command (local and persistent declared here and by all parents).
1090: func (c *Command) Flags() *flag.FlagSet {
1091: 	if c.flags == nil {
1092: 		c.flags = flag.NewFlagSet(c.Name(), flag.ContinueOnError)
1093: 		if c.flagErrorBuf == nil {
1094: 			c.flagErrorBuf = new(bytes.Buffer)
1095: 		}
1096: 		c.flags.SetOutput(c.flagErrorBuf)
1097: 	}
1098: 	return c.flags
1099: }
1100: 
1101: // LocalNonPersistentFlags are flags specific to this command which will NOT persist to subcommands.
1102: func (c *Command) LocalNonPersistentFlags() *flag.FlagSet {
1103: 	persistentFlags := c.PersistentFlags()
1104: 
1105: 	out := flag.NewFlagSet(c.Name(), flag.ContinueOnError)
1106: 	c.LocalFlags().VisitAll(func(f *flag.Flag) {
1107: 		if persistentFlags.Lookup(f.Name) == nil {
1108: 			out.AddFlag(f)
1109: 		}
1110: 	})
1111: 	return out
1112: }
1113: 
1114: // LocalFlags returns the local FlagSet specifically set in the current command.
1115: func (c *Command) LocalFlags() *flag.FlagSet {
1116: 	c.mergePersistentFlags()
1117: 
1118: 	local := flag.NewFlagSet(c.Name(), flag.ContinueOnError)
1119: 	c.lflags.VisitAll(func(f *flag.Flag) {
1120: 		local.AddFlag(f)
1121: 	})
1122: 	if !c.HasParent() {
1123: 		flag.CommandLine.VisitAll(func(f *flag.Flag) {
1124: 			if local.Lookup(f.Name) == nil {
1125: 				local.AddFlag(f)
1126: 			}
1127: 		})
1128: 	}
1129: 	return local
1130: }
1131: 
1132: // InheritedFlags returns all flags which were inherited from parents commands.
1133: func (c *Command) InheritedFlags() *flag.FlagSet {
1134: 	c.mergePersistentFlags()
1135: 
1136: 	inherited := flag.NewFlagSet(c.Name(), flag.ContinueOnError)
1137: 	local := c.LocalFlags()
1138: 
1139: 	var rmerge func(x *Command)
1140: 
1141: 	rmerge = func(x *Command) {
1142: 		if x.HasPersistentFlags() {
1143: 			x.PersistentFlags().VisitAll(func(f *flag.Flag) {
1144: 				if inherited.Lookup(f.Name) == nil && local.Lookup(f.Name) == nil {
1145: 					inherited.AddFlag(f)
1146: 				}
1147: 			})
1148: 		}
1149: 		if x.HasParent() {
1150: 			rmerge(x.parent)
1151: 		}
1152: 	}
1153: 
1154: 	if c.HasParent() {
1155: 		rmerge(c.parent)
1156: 	}
1157: 
1158: 	return inherited
1159: }
1160: 
1161: // NonInheritedFlags returns all flags which were not inherited from parent commands.
1162: func (c *Command) NonInheritedFlags() *flag.FlagSet {
1163: 	return c.LocalFlags()
1164: }
1165: 
1166: // PersistentFlags returns the persistent FlagSet specifically set in the current command.
1167: func (c *Command) PersistentFlags() *flag.FlagSet {
1168: 	if c.pflags == nil {
1169: 		c.pflags = flag.NewFlagSet(c.Name(), flag.ContinueOnError)
1170: 		if c.flagErrorBuf == nil {
1171: 			c.flagErrorBuf = new(bytes.Buffer)
1172: 		}
1173: 		c.pflags.SetOutput(c.flagErrorBuf)
1174: 	}
1175: 	return c.pflags
1176: }
1177: 
1178: // ResetFlags is used in testing.
1179: func (c *Command) ResetFlags() {
1180: 	c.flagErrorBuf = new(bytes.Buffer)
1181: 	c.flagErrorBuf.Reset()
1182: 	c.flags = flag.NewFlagSet(c.Name(), flag.ContinueOnError)
1183: 	c.flags.SetOutput(c.flagErrorBuf)
1184: 	c.pflags = flag.NewFlagSet(c.Name(), flag.ContinueOnError)
1185: 	c.pflags.SetOutput(c.flagErrorBuf)
1186: }
1187: 
1188: // HasFlags checks if the command contains any flags (local plus persistent from the entire structure).
1189: func (c *Command) HasFlags() bool {
1190: 	return c.Flags().HasFlags()
1191: }
1192: 
1193: // HasPersistentFlags checks if the command contains persistent flags.
1194: func (c *Command) HasPersistentFlags() bool {
1195: 	return c.PersistentFlags().HasFlags()
1196: }
1197: 
1198: // HasLocalFlags checks if the command has flags specifically declared locally.
1199: func (c *Command) HasLocalFlags() bool {
1200: 	return c.LocalFlags().HasFlags()
1201: }
1202: 
1203: // HasInheritedFlags checks if the command has flags inherited from its parent command.
1204: func (c *Command) HasInheritedFlags() bool {
1205: 	return c.InheritedFlags().HasFlags()
1206: }
1207: 
1208: // HasAvailableFlags checks if the command contains any flags (local plus persistent from the entire
1209: // structure) which are not hidden or deprecated.
1210: func (c *Command) HasAvailableFlags() bool {
1211: 	return c.Flags().HasAvailableFlags()
1212: }
1213: 
1214: // HasAvailablePersistentFlags checks if the command contains persistent flags which are not hidden or deprecated.
1215: func (c *Command) HasAvailablePersistentFlags() bool {
1216: 	return c.PersistentFlags().HasAvailableFlags()
1217: }
1218: 
1219: // HasAvailableLocalFlags checks if the command has flags specifically declared locally which are not hidden
1220: // or deprecated.
1221: func (c *Command) HasAvailableLocalFlags() bool {
1222: 	return c.LocalFlags().HasAvailableFlags()
1223: }
1224: 
1225: // HasAvailableInheritedFlags checks if the command has flags inherited from its parent command which are
1226: // not hidden or deprecated.
1227: func (c *Command) HasAvailableInheritedFlags() bool {
1228: 	return c.InheritedFlags().HasAvailableFlags()
1229: }
1230: 
1231: // Flag climbs up the command tree looking for matching flag.
1232: func (c *Command) Flag(name string) (flag *flag.Flag) {
1233: 	flag = c.Flags().Lookup(name)
1234: 
1235: 	if flag == nil {
1236: 		flag = c.persistentFlag(name)
1237: 	}
1238: 
1239: 	return
1240: }
1241: 
1242: // Recursively find matching persistent flag.
1243: func (c *Command) persistentFlag(name string) (flag *flag.Flag) {
1244: 	if c.HasPersistentFlags() {
1245: 		flag = c.PersistentFlags().Lookup(name)
1246: 	}
1247: 
1248: 	if flag == nil && c.HasParent() {
1249: 		flag = c.parent.persistentFlag(name)
1250: 	}
1251: 	return
1252: }
1253: 
1254: // ParseFlags parses persistent flag tree and local flags.
1255: func (c *Command) ParseFlags(args []string) (err error) {
1256: 	if c.DisableFlagParsing {
1257: 		return nil
1258: 	}
1259: 	c.mergePersistentFlags()
1260: 	err = c.Flags().Parse(args)
1261: 	return
1262: }
1263: 
1264: // Parent returns a commands parent command.
1265: func (c *Command) Parent() *Command {
1266: 	return c.parent
1267: }
1268: 
1269: func (c *Command) mergePersistentFlags() {
1270: 	var rmerge func(x *Command)
1271: 
1272: 	// Save the set of local flags
1273: 	if c.lflags == nil {
1274: 		c.lflags = flag.NewFlagSet(c.Name(), flag.ContinueOnError)
1275: 		if c.flagErrorBuf == nil {
1276: 			c.flagErrorBuf = new(bytes.Buffer)
1277: 		}
1278: 		c.lflags.SetOutput(c.flagErrorBuf)
1279: 		addtolocal := func(f *flag.Flag) {
1280: 			c.lflags.AddFlag(f)
1281: 		}
1282: 		c.Flags().VisitAll(addtolocal)
1283: 		c.PersistentFlags().VisitAll(addtolocal)
1284: 	}
1285: 	rmerge = func(x *Command) {
1286: 		if !x.HasParent() {
1287: 			flag.CommandLine.VisitAll(func(f *flag.Flag) {
1288: 				if x.PersistentFlags().Lookup(f.Name) == nil {
1289: 					x.PersistentFlags().AddFlag(f)
1290: 				}
1291: 			})
1292: 		}
1293: 		if x.HasPersistentFlags() {
1294: 			x.PersistentFlags().VisitAll(func(f *flag.Flag) {
1295: 				if c.Flags().Lookup(f.Name) == nil {
1296: 					c.Flags().AddFlag(f)
1297: 				}
1298: 			})
1299: 		}
1300: 		if x.HasParent() {
1301: 			rmerge(x.parent)
1302: 		}
1303: 	}
1304: 
1305: 	rmerge(c)
1306: }
